reasoning:
1. March 9, 2025: FIDE updates ratings monthly on the 1st. The Wikipedia page will still reflect the March 1 list (where Arjun is exactly 2777). 2777 is not 1% greater than 2777, so the probability is effectively 0 (submitted as 0.02).
2. April 1, 2025: To hit +1% (an official rating of 2805), Arjun would need to gain 28 rating points in a single month. This requires a mathematically massive winning streak against elite or sub-elite GMs without any losses. Given he just lost a lot of points at Tata Steel, bouncing back +28 ELO in one rating period is extremely unlikely (0.04).
3. May 31, 2025: Slightly more time to accumulate points over the April and May rating lists. However, reaching 2805 official rating has only been done by ~15 players in history, and climbing back to this after falling to 2777 takes serious time and consistent high performance (0.09).
4. August 29, 2025: Over a 6-month period, a volatile and active player like Arjun (who reached 2809 live in Nov 2024) has a small but realistic chance to hit a hot streak and clear 2805. Probability rises (0.18).
5. March 2, 2026: Over a 1-year horizon, if his true strength continues to improve and his Tata Steel slump was an outlier, he has a reasonable chance of breaking 2805 legitimately. Increasing uncertainty over this longer horizon pulls the estimate closer toward 0.5 (0.35).
